{"id":"gtm-0s6","title":"gt feed --mesh: cross-mesh activity feed","description":"Add a --mesh flag to gt feed that pulls activity from the DoltHub mesh tables (activity_log, messages, peers) and merges them into the existing feed TUI.

Data sources in DoltHub (deepwork/gt-agent-mail):
- activity_log: 2577 entries, has gt_id/action/subject/body/created_at
- messages: cross-GT mail with from_gt/to_gt/subject/body/priority
- peers: gt_id/role/status/last_seen

Implementation:
1. Add new event source in internal/tui/feed/ (e.g. mesh_source.go)
2. Query /tmp/mesh-sync-clone Dolt DB for activity_log + messages
3. Convert to feed events with appropriate symbols for mesh activity
4. Add --mesh flag to feed.go cmd definition
5. When --mesh is set, include mesh_source in multi_source aggregation
6. Show peer online status in agent tree panel

Existing feed code:
- Cmd: gastown/internal/cmd/feed.go
- TUI: gastown/internal/tui/feed/ (14 Go files)
- Sources: bd activity, .events.jsonl, MQ events
- New source would follow same pattern as mq_source.go","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"prathamonchain@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T11:45:20Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-11T11:45:20Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Implementation complete. Enhanced mesh-feed.sh with activity_log+messages+peers from DoltHub, added symbols/filtering/JSON output. Created mesh-feed-bridge.sh that exports mesh events to .events.jsonl for gt feed integration. Bridge auto-runs on mesh-sync. Note: gt binary is compiled Go, cannot add --mesh flag directly. Instead: (1) gt mesh feed is the standalone cross-mesh feed, (2) bridge writes mesh events into .events.jsonl so gt feed shows them natively."}
